title: ciconium × hybridum
parts: Ciconium × hybridum has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to produce various medicinal compounds. The flowers of Ciconium × hybridum are known to contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of this plant contain saponins, such as glycosides, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The roots of Ciconium × hybridum contain alkaloids, like berberine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and infections.
side_effects: Ciconium × hybridum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can lead to nausea, headaches, and dizziness. Additionally, ingesting the plant can cause stomach upset, diarrhea, and vomiting, as its toxic alkaloids can interfere with the body's natural digestive processes. Improper use can also trigger anaphylaxis, a potentially life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention.
